method: 5-persona committee (Comp Analyst, Trend Scout, Value Investor, Velocity Flipper, Risk Manager) + chair synthesis; JC removed Hurts and the 3 already-stocked picks (Chase/Golden/Hampton)
constraints: no NY Giants, no Jaxson Dart, no Eagles; fit $3-15 modern-single store; no pile-on of deep inventory

targets.why: Endorsed by all five personas (Numbers 9, Churn 8.5, Brakes 8, Buzz 6.5, plus implied). Elite dual-threat RB, top-of-band margin (median ~$14.49), 10K depth without oversupply, parallels hold instead of collapsing. RB scarcity in JC's book and zero current inventory make this a clean diversifier. No vetoes anywhere.
targets.target: Prizm/Select RCs and colored parallels at the top of band ($10-15); base Donruss/Optic at $4-8. Lions team-collector demand supports velocity.

targets.why: Brakes' lowest-regret add (9), Buzz (9), Churn (7.5). Record-setting rookie TE (112/1,194), zero injury flags, huge Raiders fanbase, and fills the thinnest category in JC's book (only Kittle at 13). Proven $5-15 velocity, parallels clear within days. Hold's veto is value-preference only; quality consensus overrides.
targets.target: Prizm/Donruss RC base + colored parallels (Silver, Pink, numbered) at $5-15. Topps Chrome where available. TE category is under-stocked so depth here is additive, not redundant.

targets.why: Numbers (8.5), Churn (8), Brakes (8). Best non-saturated band fill (26/60, median ~$6.47, 5.8K depth). TE is thin in JC's book and LaPorta is the rare TE whose RCs actually move AND hold. Sourcing-friendly $6-9. Second TE add diversifies the weakest position without redundancy (different team/price tier than Bowers). No vetoes.
targets.target: Prizm/Select/Donruss RCs at $6-9; colored parallels to $12. Cheap-in sourcing, fast flips. Pairs with Gibbs as a Lions team-demand cluster.

targets.why: Explosive dual-threat RB, top-of-band margin and reliable demand; keeps real RB exposure in the list. Not in current inventory.
targets.target: Prizm/Select RCs + colored parallels $8-15; base Optic/Donruss $4-8.

targets.why: Numbers (8.5), Churn (8), Brakes (7.5). Proven WR1, 12K deep, reliable in-band realization (~$6.75) with low boom-bust spread and zero current inventory. Steady velocity hold, prime age. Third Lions name (with Gibbs/LaPorta) builds a team-collector cluster that compounds demand. No vetoes.
targets.target: Prizm/Select/Donruss singles at $6-7 realized; colored parallels to $12. Reliable steady mover, not a spike play. Source consistently.

targets.why: Churn (8.5), Numbers (7). The most liquid market in the pool (~37K listings), constant national search volume keeps cheap base/parallels turning daily, zero JC inventory. Brakes' veto concedes the turnover is real (calls it bottom-of-band churn) and Buzz's veto only says it's not a buzz spike, neither disputes velocity. For a high-volume single-card store, daily liquidity is exactly the engine; placed mid-rank to respect the thinner band fill.
targets.target: Cheap base + low parallels at $3-8 for constant turnover; bigger colored/numbered parallels to $15 when sourced cheap. Volume play, keep cost basis low.

targets.why: Emerged as a true WR1 and Herbert favorite; rising hobby attention without the saturation of bigger 2024 WR names. Zero current inventory.
targets.target: Colored Prizm/Chrome parallels $3-12; base RCs cheap-in.

targets.why: Buzz (8.5), Hold (7). Seattle's clear WR1 whose RC pricing still lags true alpha receivers (the inefficiency), with a live SB LX reactive-buzz catalyst. Two personas with distinct theses (trend heat + value lag) converge. Brakes vetoes as fading post-SB buzz, but the underlying WR1 role is real and the value case stands independent of the spike; ranked lower to respect that risk. Different team/profile from the other WR adds, so no redundancy.
targets.target: Buy colored Prizm/Chrome parallels at $5-15 during the post-SB attention window; also accumulate quiet base RCs on the value-lag thesis. Move spike-sensitive inventory faster.

targets.why: Hold (8). Top-2 fantasy TE selling at mid-TE money because TEs always reprice last, the position discount is the inefficiency. Steady appreciation as it narrows, and JC has near-zero TE depth. Chosen over the heavily-vetoed value/rookie names (MHJ, Richardson, Tyson, Mendoza, Tate all drew multiple hard vetoes). Rounds out a deliberate three-TE thrust into JC's thinnest category with a value-tier, single-persona-but-unopposed-on-quality pick.
targets.target: Prizm/Select/Donruss singles at $4-9; colored parallels to $12. Accumulate quietly while the TE discount holds. Cardinals offense uncertainty is the only knock, so source selectively.

targets.why: Established Texans WR1, steady $3-8 realized demand, fills a zero-Texans-WR gap. Low-ceiling but genuinely low-regret liquidity.

honorable_mentions: Justin Jefferson (WR, Vikings) — clean restock, you're thin at 8
Emeka Egbuka (WR, Buccaneers) — rookie with real sell-through
Jared Goff (QB, Lions) — extends the Lions cluster if Herbert is 'done'
Kyren Williams (RB, Rams) — workhorse, steady velocity
Bo Nix (QB, Broncos) — deepen if wanted (already hold ~7)
